title: Retail Therapy Steals $3,200 — And Stores Plan It
hook: You think shopping cheers you up. Stores engineer that feeling — then bill you $3,200 a year for it.
angle: Retailers deliberately target emotionally vulnerable moments using behavioral data — turning your sadness into their revenue, and 75% of those buys are regretted by morning
audience: Anyone who has ever stress-shopped, boredom-bought, or woken up with buyer's remorse on something they ordered at midnight
key_numbers: $3,200 average annual emotional spending per American
62% of Americans admit to retail therapy
30% more spending when stressed vs. calm
70% of impulse urges vanish after a 48-hour pause
